entry.title: Re: Authentication Issues occur when sending mail to outl...
entry.content: Hallo Ayechan, thanks for reaching out to us, Are you still encountering an issue?

entry.title: Re: SAP IRPA/Build Get Open Workbook List gives more records
entry.content:
Hello Liska,

Thank you for reaching out to us. As a best practice, we recommend starting by closing all open Excel instances and initiating the loop afresh. If a workbook is already open, there is a possibility that Excel may update it incorrectly. Additionally, please ensure that the workbook path is properly maintained.

Let us know if you need further assistance.

entry.title: AP CPI integration - Query filter conditions and null date&time issue!
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am trying to get business partner list of records that created or modified within specific date and time duration.

Example: get all business partners from system that created between 1-1-2025 10:00AM to 1/2/2025 11:00AM  or last changed on 1-1-2025 10:00AM to 1/2/2025 11:00PM

Notes: -

1. SAP business partners thurogh CPI integration has 4 fields for creation date or updated date:

"CreationDate": "2025-01-20T00:00:00.000",
"Customer": "21600031",
"LastChangeDate": "2025-01-21T00:00:00.000",
"BusinessPartner": "21600031",
"CreationTime": "1970-01-01T11:11:16.000",
"LastChangeTime": "1970-01-01T08:45:20.000",

2. LastChangeDate or LastChangeTime will be null in SAP system unless the record is not changed.

My CPI Query option filter is:

$select=Customer,BusinessPartner,Supplier,CreationDate,CreationTime,LastChangeDate,LastChangeTime&$filter=CreationDate ge datetime'${property.CreationDate}'
and (LastChangeDate ne null) or LastChangeDate ge datetime'${property.LastChangeDate}'

when try to consume the cpi return bad request.

Please support by write the right query option filter in order to get all business partners within a specific creation or updating date&time and ignoring the null last change datetime records.

refer to screenshot.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
Thanks for you great blog Abhijit_Shinde which will be helpful.

I would like to have some Insite of cxml invoice posting from Non ERP system through API posting and getting the invoice status update (accepted or rejected) for cxml invoice through API for Non ERP system. 

could you please provide some Insite on the requirement which will greatly helpful.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations

entry.title: Unable to generate PRs for operations in a Work Order
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

A client is having an issue involving the creation of purchase requisitions for a subset of positions while creating a maintenance/work order in PRD.

As shown in image, the field Res/PurcRq is set to "From release" for both operation 0020 and 0040 when, instead, they should be set to Inmediately, as shown for positions 0030 and 0050.

The purchase requisitions are created interspersedly.

The user is unable to modify this field.

A deeper view into operation/position 0020 shows no creation of a PR.

Meanwhile, operation 0030 does, indeed, shows a PR being created.

We tried creating a corrective maintenance order in QAS following the same procedure, not having the same error. It's only happening in PRD.

Customizing for both QAS and PRD.

Any thought on how to proceed?

Thanks in advance,

Sebastián

entry.title: Re: MDG Cloud Ready Mode: Custom Workflow template in SAP build process automation with S/4HANA
entry.content:
HI Frederik,

The out of the box inbox application of MDG cloud ready mode doesn't provide any details which you have mentioned above. You can configure few extra parameters coming from payload like the process ID, type of process, name of the business partner in the build process automation notification step.

I mentioned in my earlier comments that MDG cloud edition inbox app shows lot more details than what MDG cloud ready mode inbox app shows right now. I am sure it's a work in progress and might be enhanced in future releases.

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: Get all the values in a single line
entry.content:
One possible solution is to use the cross table with the following variables:

v_Standby_C:   =If ([Standby Date] >= RelativeDate(CurrentDate();-3;DayPeriod) And [Standby Date] <= (RelativeDate(CurrentDate();6;DayPeriod ))) Then [Standby Date] 

v_X:   =If ([Standby Date] = [v_Standby_C]) In ([Airtel];[v_Standby_C]) Then "X"

..

I hope it helps and this is what you are looking for

Regards,

Niyati

entry.title: Re: Physical inv & batch management
entry.content:
Hello @lukkurylo 

As per note 1502321 - Physical Inventory Posting Block Removed

If a material is batch managed then it is possible to make a goods movement posting for the material but for a different batch.

This is because the actual posting block is set on the batch table MCHB (SPERC) and not on table MARD for the material.

So it is the correct system behavior. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content:
Hi @RoosReijers , Issue with  tree security role assignments will be addresses in next release.

Thanks,

Hari

Vasanth.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration Connection IP
entry.content:
Sorry for a really late answer.
Today there exist APIs for that, which you can build into your flow
ipify - A Simple Public IP Address API

There is most likely also other services that does the same thing.

entry.title: Re: Is Seamless Planning between SAC and SAP Datasphere a...
entry.content:
Hi @wsamuel 

The seamless planning functionality is available only to the customers and partners who joined the seamles planning controlled release with the SAC QRC4 2024. More information here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/seamless-planning-between-sap-analytics-cloud-and-sap-datasphere-is-coming/ba-p/13877664

The general availability is planned with SAC QRC1 2025. However, access to the functionality with QRC1 is subject to the SAC tenant running on Hana Cloud. More information on Hana Cloud migration is here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-analytics-cloud-infrastructure-is-moving-to-sap-hana-cloud/ba-p/13573478

entry.title: Re: On the Urgency of Crypto-Agility
entry.content:
Thanks for the thoughtful article!

Can you clarify whether you're proposing the Crypto Broker to be a separate program, a separate node in a network, a separate thread etc., or is it just a logically distinct component of the application, e.g. a separate Java class?

thanks

entry.title: Striking a Balance: Navigating the Open-Source Tightrope in a World of Evolving Threats - Part 1
entry.content:
By Philip Engelmartin, Technical Advisor, Office of the CSO

 

Understanding the Multifaceted Risks of Open-Source Reliance

Open-source software (OSS) is integral to the software development landscape, renowned for its perceived cost savings, innovation potential, and community collaboration. Nonetheless, heavy reliance on OSS introduces significant risks and complexities that organizations must thoughtfully navigate. This article delves into the numerous risks tied to OSS and underscores the necessity of addressing these challenges to build a more secure and resilient software supply chain.

The Open an Collaborative Nature of OSS: Both Asset and Liability

The open and collaborative nature of OSS development is a double-edged sword. While it drives rapid innovation and promotes community engagement, it also opens doors to vulnerabilities that malicious actors can exploit. These threats extend beyond simple coding errors and manifest in various insidious forms:

Malicious Contributors

One of the most significant risks is the potential for threat actors to disguise themselves as legitimate contributors and inject malicious code directly into OSS projects. Given the decentralized and often anonymous nature of OSS contributions, it becomes challenging to properly vet contributors. Consequently, the introduction and downstream propagation of malicious code become significant risks, compromising the security and integrity of software. The lack of formalized vetting processes in many OSS communities exacerbates this issue, making it easier for bad actors to slip under the radar.

Hidden Backdoors and Logic Bombs

Malicious code can be cunningly hidden within seemingly benign functionalities, activated by a remote command, a specific date, or an event. These concealed threats can leak sensitive data, disrupt operations, or provide unauthorized access to critical systems, potentially causing extensive damage before detection and remediation. The complexity and vast size of many OSS projects make it easier to hide such malicious code, increasing the risk that it will go unnoticed until it is too late.

Supply Chain Vulnerabilities

OSS projects often depend on a complex web of other open-source components, creating a vast attack surface. A single compromised component can trigger cascading effects throughout the ecosystem. Adding to this complexity is the issue of crypto agility - the ability to swiftly adapt to changes in cryptographic algorithms and implementations. Many OSS components rely on cryptography for security but may not be designed for easy upgrades or transitions, leaving software vulnerable [1]. The interconnectedness of OSS projects and their dependencies makes it difficult to track and manage vulnerabilities effectively.

Zero-Day Exploits and Public Disclosure

Vulnerabilities found in OSS are often disclosed publicly, giving malicious actors a window of opportunity to exploit these flaws before patches are developed and deployed. This race against time leaves organizations vulnerable to attacks that exploit these known weaknesses, highlighting the critical importance of prompt and effective vulnerability management [2]. Public disclosure also means that organizations must be ready to act quickly to mitigate risks, which can be challenging given the widespread use of OSS components.

The Log4Shell Incident: A Wake-Up Call

The Log4Shell vulnerability [3], discovered in the widely used Log4j logging library, serves as a stark reminder of the extensive impact a single OSS vulnerability can have. Affecting thousands of organizations globally, this incident underscores the urgent need for a more secure and controlled approach to software development. It emphasizes the necessity of proactive measures and robust security practices to protect against such widespread vulnerabilities that can devastate an organization's security posture.

Unpredictable Lifecycles and Support Gaps

The decentralized and often volunteer-driven nature of OSS development can lead to unpredictable project lifecycles, creating significant challenges for organizations that rely on these components:

Maintenance Gaps and Security Risks

Unsupported or abandoned OSS components are less likely to receive timely security updates, leaving software vulnerable to exploits. This lack of maintenance extends to cryptographic components as well. Without active development, OSS projects may fall behind in implementing the latest cryptographic standards or addressing newly discovered vulnerabilities in existing algorithms, exacerbating security risks. Organizations must invest significant resources in monitoring and maintaining these components to mitigate these risks.

Compatibility Issues and Integration Challenges

Combining OSS components with different versions and dependencies can lead to compatibility issues, requiring considerable engineering effort and potentially impacting software stability. These challenges necessitate rigorous testing and continuous integration practices to ensure seamless and stable software performance. Organizations must be prepared to dedicate substantial resources to managing these integration challenges, which can be particularly burdensome in complex software environments.

Lack of Service Level Agreements (SLAs)

OSS typically does not come with the formal support and SLAs provided by commercial software. This absence leaves organizations to rely on internal resources or community support, which can be inconsistent and unreliable, particularly when dealing with critical issues that demand immediate resolution. Organizations must often invest in building internal expertise to manage these support gaps, which can be a significant drain on resources and expertise.

Conclusion

While OSS offers many benefits, the risks associated with its use can be substantial and often underestimated. Organizations must carefully assess these risks and take proactive steps to mitigate them. Understanding the multifaceted risks is the first step in developing a comprehensive strategy to build a more secure and resilient software supply chain. This approach requires organizations to strike a delicate balance between leveraging the advantages of OSS and implementing robust security measures to protect against evolving threats.

Stay tuned for the next article in this series, where we explore the complexities of OSS licensing and geopolitical influences.

[1] https://sweet32.info/  
[2] https://www.threatdown.com/blog/five-years-later-heartbleed-vulnerability-still-unpatched/  
[3] https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/cve-2021-44228

entry.title: Re: Form template name cant be initial error while output...
entry.content: The PDF file for revise payment proposal is not being generated. Please assist to check.

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content:
Hi Pascal,

this is not possible. 

Which release are you using? With the fiori monitoring UIs we have introduced key field functions. With the key field function you can add a forward navigation based on the content of a key field (the feature is only available for AIFs Fiori Monitoring UIs).

Best Regards

Britta

entry.title: Re: Error when uploading Data in the Manage Lookup Table...
entry.content:
Hi Lukas,

the original error message indicates that ranges are used. 
In your screenshot you've provided only the price condition columns.
Could you maybe post a screenshot with all columns and the row which produces the error? You might want to blur some sensitive information like product codes if required.

For the price condition in your screenshot I can tell you that Tier-based price conditions require also the columns
price (Amount Currency), price (Pricing Unit Value) and price (Pricing Unit UoM or Currency), e.g.

entry.title: Re: Replace a Model in the Optimized (Unified) Experience
entry.content:
@anilmuppa9  Could you please let me know more details on that feature request ? It will really help.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: 32bit crystal reports .net runtime discontinued in De...
entry.content: Thank you, Don, for your response and it was very helpful in clarifying how to move over to 64bit using VS2019. We are definitely migrating but the issue for us is the timing. We may not be able to perform the conversion with ample time for implementation and testing this year, so we are really looking for some kind of assurance if we continue utilizing the 32 bit runtime into 2026 as we complete the conversion. In my experience, discontinued or out of support components can continue to function as expected assuming the runtime environment stays the same. I am assuming unless SAP has implemented a 'kill switch' for the 32bit runtime that will shut it down exactly in Dec 2025, the same can be said for this. However, also knowing how things work, SAP probably can't give any guarantees past Dec 2025 regarding the 32bit runtime. So what I'm looking for is a statement from SAP that says past Dec 2025 we cannot give any guarantees, however, there is no immediate kill switch on the 32bit runtime and as far as we know things should continue to work given your runtimes environments stay the same. With this statement, that would give us an additional time into 2026 where we can complete the conversion. Please advise and thank you again for your assistance, Don.

entry.title: Re: Configuration Transport Center: New way to view, download and transport configurations
entry.content:
Thanks @hariprasadgud ,

For splitting bundle permissions I have created an influence request, https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/339319.

It is auto assigned to Fannie Guan as it relates to RBP.

entry.title: Re: list of items field is not displaying (SAP b1)
entry.content:
Hello,

You can try also, in the List Item screen, click on menu "View -> Restore Column Width".

Maybe the column is there, but with the width = zero.

Hope it helps

entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Hi @nickv23 
Please check our Staying certified website where you find the FAQ

Do I need to have a subscription to SAP Learning Hub to stay certified?
Yes. To stay certified, you must have a subscription to the enhanced and redesigned SAP Learning Hub. With one single subscription, you can get access to all resources you need to prepare for certifications, take the certification exams with four included certification attempts and stay certified with access to stay certified assessments and preparation materials. You can purchase a subscription on SAP Learning Hub or by contacting your SAP representative. SAP PartnerEdge members can learn more about and subscribe to SAP Learning Hub, partner edition.

Alternatively, if you don’t have an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you still have the option to purchase and retake an SAP Certification exam every year.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: A Complete Guide to Comparing Versions in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Excellent blog, very helpful to see the different options to compare versions in SAC Planning. The restricted measures with constant selection was a good trick.

This functionality has been is a huge value add for my team during our annual planning processes. 

Thank you for taking the time to share.

entry.title: Re: Entities Created in SAP CAP UI but Fields Have Null V...
entry.content:
The issue was with the "annotation.cds" file, specifically in the value of idocTypeConfiguration.segList.qualifier. The correct value should simply be qualifier.

To resolve this, I had to remove idocTypeConfiguration.segList from all relevant attributes.

entry.title: Re: SAC Add-in for Excel
entry.content: SAC plugin is a lot worst than Analysis for Office, and slow to refresh data... I would prefer 100 times Analysis fo Office. If SAP will not allow that Analysis for Office connect to SAC models to consume data then it's very annoying.. we only use it to check data in models...

entry.title: Re: What you need to get and stay certified
entry.content:
Hi @Marm 
Please check our How to get certified? 
Threr you find th FAQ
14. Failed your exam?
You can take an SAP Certification exam up to three (3) times. If you do not pass an exam after three (3) attempts, you will need to re-register for the next release of the exam. If you do not pass the next release after three (3) attempts, you will not be able to retake the exam until the next edition is released, normally within 1 year.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Error when uploading Data in the Manage Lookup Table...

Its all the same customer id and just different rateElements. I thought that maybe some of the entrys are mandatory but i dont know which.

entry.title: Any possible side effects to using TOUCHSRC?
entry.content:
Hi all,

Recently we had some dumps with LOAD_PROGRAM_TABLE_MISMATCH related to a column added to a standard table. There are some SAP Notes related to this and one of the recommendations is running the program TOUCHSRC for fixing the program that gave out the error.

My question: Is there any possible problem or side effect using this program (TOUCHSRC)? My main concern is that this would be executed in a production system.

TIA,

Luis

entry.title: Re: Entities Created in SAP CAP UI but Fields Have Null Values and Data Not Inserted in the DB
entry.content: No i don't have handler logic associated with the create event

Marm

entry.title: Re: Replace a Model in the Optimized (Unified) Experience
entry.content:
@ChintS I've requested the SAC development team to provide an option to replace Models in SAC Stories for different data Source types such as SAP HANA Live model to SAP Datasphere analytical model, so that we do not have to rebuild the reports that are based on a HANA view. 

The last update I got was a month ago and it says "This is HIGH Priority topic for Development team and they are working on this topic. We can expect some information in 2025 roadmap".

entry.title: How can i expose my BTP CAPM application built with nodejs to external system
entry.content:
Hi

I want to create a CAPM application which will create database entries into Postgresql (BTP backing service) and read data from postgresql DB.  I want this application to be accessible from ABAP system (through request parameter i need to perform DB operations).

Thanks 

Shankar

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process Intelligence
entry.content: Why is it that after successfully transferring data from ServiceNow to SAP Process Intelligence and successfully creating the table to use for analysis, the cycle time doesn't show up in my investigation discovery?

entry.title: Re: Retrieve metadata failed because the OData server ret...
entry.content: I suspect the issue is in the SAP Cloud Connector trust to the on-premise system.  This is done through the use of short lived certificates and requires setup both in SCC and the on-premise system through CERTRULE and possibly STRUST if using self-signed certificates from the SCC.  I would start by verifying your SCC configuration to the host and associated settings.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA for Product Compliance: "Reverse" Outdated supplier status
entry.content:

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Does RSTRAN table exist in BW Bridge?
entry.content:
Hello to everyone.

In one of my project, we are looking for the counts of BW objects(Adso, DS, TRFN, etc.) available in BW Bridge.

In BW/BW/4HANA such information were available in standard tables like RSDS, RSTRAN, etc. 

Does these tables are still relevant in case of BW bridge? if yes, where and how can I find this at database(Schema) level?

entry.title: Global assignment - business rule
entry.content:
Hello dear experts,

We have initiated shot-term assignments for some colleagues who will be staying in Germany for a period of time and have noticed that on the Home profile, the employee group should have automatically changed from Employee to Expatriate and did not. 

We created a business rule but seems to not work as expected. 

Any guidance would be appreciated.

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: Wildcard Character for WEBi Search
entry.content:
...MATCHES PATTERN... and 'AB%'  should work.

What is your database from which you are pulling data?

Matches Pattern operator | SAP Help Portal

is there a way to replicate work time efforts (absences / reservations) created by a technician in FSM to SAP ECC?

Thanks in advance and best regards,

Max

entry.title: Re: Component Allocation for multiple alternative bom in...
entry.content:
Hi, 

similar issue here. 

What we are missing is the window that is visible on your Screenshot, where it is possible to select from maintained BOM alternative. 

We have a Retail System if that should make a difference - 2 BOM Alternatives maintained, but the system always picks BOM alternative 1 with no possibility to intervene/change to BOM version 2. 

We cannot deactivate BOM alternative 1, because they could get used at the same time. 
Is it because the affected material is a phantom assembly?

entry.title: SAPLSLVC_FULLSCREEN Not showing buttions ALV
entry.content:
Hi , 

After sap upgrade from s4/ hana to cloud version , when executing specific ALV Report , some buttons in the toolbar don't appear even though all buttons are active in the painter , what could be the problem , my GUI Status is ZSTANDRAD copy of standard of SAPLSLVC_FULLSCREEN GUI Status

entry.title: Re: What is meant by call off Purchase orders?
entry.content:
Hello @AAkash1 

Call-off purchase orders are know in SAP as contract release orders. These are purchase orders created against a contract - SAP Help Creating a Contract Release Order

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content: Hi Britta,

entry.title: From Old to New: Watch Transformation in Action
entry.content:
Transformation is Key: Say goodbye to the old and hello to the new ⚡️

In the Utilities industry, challenges are constant and efficiency is everything. Check out what transformation can do for your business:

1. Build stronger connections 

Discover how SAP solutions for Customer Experience, including SAP Service Cloud for Utilities, transform the way you engage with customers at every stage of their journey. From providing personalized service to handling complex service requests and orders with ease, this solution makes it simpler to overview your data and keep your customers satisfied.

💡Want to learn more? Check out these insightful blogs and articles to see what is now possible: 

  • Working with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Add-on for Utilities: Master Data Replication Part I 
  • Working with SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Add-on for Utilities

2. Optimize operations 

Experience how easily it is now to enhance workforce management and improve customer satisfaction in your utility company by digitizing service and asset management processes with SAP solutions for Service and Asset Management, with a ready-to-use platform to automate workforce scheduling and efficiency with AI support. 

💡Looking for more details? Check out in-depth resources: 

  • SAP Field Service Management: GenAI, Enhanced User Experience, and Improved Integration 
  • Enhance Your Maintenance and Field Technicians' Efficiency and Productivity with SAP Dynamic Forms

3. Make real-time decisions with real-time data

In the data-driven future of utilities, SAP S/4HANA Utilities and FIORI Apps offer a clear, real-time view of your processes. From metering and billing to invoicing, easily track progress, spot issues, and make quick decisions with user-friendly visuals - all of this in just three easy-to-follow levels, replacing the need for multiple steps. 

💡Want to dive deeper? Explore the latest updates and innovations:

  • SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS2: What's in it for the Utilities Industry?
  • SAP S/4HANA Utilities Demo Series 

Discover What's Possible...

Step into the future with solutions that make a real difference and embrace the benefits:

  • Improved efficiency and productivity 
  • Increased agility and flexibility
  • Enhanced customer experience 
  • Competitive advantage
  • Latest user experience 

Transformation is more than just change - it's about making your life and work easier. Watch this video series to see how our latest, user-friendly solutions can simplify daily tasks for you, your teams, and your customers.

Stay tuned for more to come! ⚡

 

Best regards,

Your ️SAP Energy & Utilities Brand Awareness team

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content: Hi Britta, we are on S/4HANA 2020 Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Forecast schedule lines vs. Planned schedule lines in...
entry.content:
Thank yo for your request, @alaintgkm ,

please have a look at the flow diagram of business scope item 3NR which shows how forecasts vs planning data are being processed in SAP best practices.

Also, please have a good look at the Accelerators of this scope item, mainly the set-up instructions and test script:

 

I got to the scope item in the SAP for Me Process Navigator via the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for the API you had mentioned, API Reference | Delivery Schedule of Sales Scheduling Agreement – Receive, Update (B2B) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub which has many more details about the API proper and its business context.

I noticed your request had been lingering in the Community for a bit. In future, please use tag "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition" for respective requests as this tag is monitored regularly by our experts. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace -Send shipment status to customer/buyer using easy r
entry.content:
Hello Elaine,

See my answers,

1. I am not sure that is possible in an e-mail. What is the use case of button Vs hyperlink?

2. Use place holder   ${BP_LOGO}. This will replace with logo image from 'Manage Business Profile' app.

3. You can have images(static) added in Manage Notification Template app email template

4. Use Editor capability within Manage Notification Template app

entry.title: Re: Replicate SAP FSM WorkTime (Reservations/Absences) to...
entry.content: As of today, this is the only way to handle WTP, reservations, and absences. However, there is some work happening on the integration front; please check the roadmap for more details. SAP Road Map Explorer

entry.title: 360 reviews grouped
entry.content: In AVC we need to show the results of the 360 reviews grouped by role in the summary report that comes with the form. Have any of you managed to configure the aggregated result?

entry.title: Re: FSM - Planning & Dispatching - Activities List
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

Yes, the behavior you described aligns with the design of the Planning & Dispatching function. The Activities tab was specifically designed to display only those activities that pertain to the chosen planning scenario in the Dispatching board. Therefore, currently there is no available setting to view all activities regardless of the planning scenario selected.

However, we are open to customer feedback and suggestions for improvement. Therefore, if you would like the ability to view all activities in the Activities tab, we would encourage you to open an influence at https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/1608. Here, you can submit with a use case for this possible amendment. Your suggestion will then be reviewed and potentially considered for inclusion in future software updates.

 

Thanks & Best Regards,
Ziad Mamlouk

entry.title: Re: Step by Step process to download EDIFACT XSDs from Integration Advisor for B2B Integration in CI
entry.content:
Thanks Adharsh,

 Any idea why am I getting "service not found" while creating MIG. I have followed the same steps you mentioned above. 

Any guidance would be of great help!

Cheers!

entry.title: Impact of ignoreErrors on Active Index in SolrIndexConfig
entry.content:
In the SolrIndexConfig, there is a property called ignoreErrors. According to the documentation, this property allows the index to continue running even if failures occur.

My question is: Will setting ignoreErrors=true and running an index-Job also change the active index, even if the index is not complete?
Or will it simply run through and log all the errors without making any changes to the active index?

entry.content: For us it works, so far no problems with 2025

entry.content: Hello Stevica, It's a great tool and helped me a lot. Thank you very much. Best regards

entry.title: Re: Error VPK1310 in DP93 - Intercompany Resource Related...
entry.content: If you are in S4, carry out code corrections as recommended in SAP note 3277686

entry.title: Re: SAP Universal ID S users - issue
entry.content: @alessia_pozzi Thank you for the update! I am glad to hear it got sorted 🙂

entry.title: Re: Reference field for doc. Type KX doesn't appear in FBL1N
entry.content:
Resolution

Please follow the below steps.

1. Regenerate the structure POSTAB.

  •      For FBL*N, Call transaction SE38 and execute the program RFPOSXEXTEND.
  •      For FAGLL03, Call transaction SE37, enter the function module ITEM_STRUC_EXTENSION and execute it with the following parameters:

                BASIC_STRUCNAME       -->    FAGLPOSY
                EXT_STRUCNAME           -->    FAGLPOSYEXT
                EXT_FIELDS_TABNAME   -->    T021S
                I_LSTCL                              -->   D
                X_TRANSPORT                -->    ' '

2. Clear the buffer to get the values populated.

  • Call transaction SE38 and execute the program BALVBUFDEL.

Now re-run the transactions FBL*N/FAGLL03, check for those special fields available for selection in change layout and see the values displayed for the same.

entry.title: Re: How can I add code formatting in a comment in response to a question on the SAP Community page?
entry.content: To confirm quickly. Enabling the full editor for comments is on our enhancement request list.

entry.title: Re: Copy control is missing AF. Message -VL473
entry.content: If you were trying to create delivery with reference to a quotation, then, it is not possible.  You need to create a sales order with reference to the quotation and then, with reference to that sales order, system will allow to create delivery.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Treat to meet you. I live in the US outside of Philadelphia. My role is a community advocate. Your topics are fascinating. Be sure to join Americas | Location-Based Groups | SAP Community and the many interest groups.
Also don't miss the upcoming virtual event: SAP Business Unleashed in 2025 | Virtual Event

entry.title: Re: Setting up favorites in SAP Build WorkZone - Standard...
entry.content: That's correct, but only for classic display modes. The new site experience, using spaces, pages and UI integration cards, unfortunately is not yet able to use any personalization features.

entry.title: Re: A warm welcome to our new SAP Champions in January 2025
entry.content: Congratulations, and welcome to all new SAP Champions! 🎉

entry.title: Understanding the Recruiting to Onboarding Mapping and troubleshooting
entry.content:
This article was built together with @CarolinaCLeite. Here, we summarized the main recommendations and suggestions for Recruit-To-Hire Data Mapping.

For the data flow from Recruiting to Onboarding, we need to take into account some assumptions:

  • Onboarding shares the Succession Data Model with EC
  • Fields marked as required in the Succession Data Model are required in the mapping

In this article, you can learn how to set up the mapping properly. 

To trigger Onboarding from Recruiting, it's only necessary to map a subset of info needed to start the Onboarding processes. However, the mapping is based on the ONB/EC data model, and all fields required in the data model will be required for mapping. 

To avoid adding these in Recruiting, where it would make no sense to have some of that specific data, you can follow several strategies.

 

First Scenario:

Use person types to make the fields required in the UI but not for the mapping

Example: "Preferred Name" should be derived via business rule in ONB/EC, or be required in the UI, but not mapped from Recruiting

Step 1: Make the field ‘Preferred Name’ in personalinfo non mandatory in the BCUI 

Step 2: Create an Employee Person Type and an Onboardee Person Type, and make the field mandatory

Step 3: Preferred Name is not required in Recruit-To-Hire Data Mapping

Not all portlets allow the creation of person types. Below you can find a list of not supported ones. For these cases, review the second and third scenarios and "other".

 

HRIS ElementAction/Notes
compInfo There is no employee type 
Job Relationship TypeThere is no employee type
payComponent (Recurring & Non-Recurring)There is no employee type 
User Accounts There is no employee type

 

Second Scenario:

Make the field not required in the BCUI. Create a rule to change the properties of the field to make it required in the UI.

Step 1: Make these fields not required in the portlet in the BCUI (as in the first scenario)

Step 2: Create a rule to set the "required" property to "true"

For more info on how to set up the rule in ONB/EC data model, please refer to the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/using-business-rules-to-set-field-visibility-required-attributes/ba-p/13501339 

 

Third Scenario:

Create dummy fields in Recruiting and use these in the mapping.

Step 1: Create both a text field and a date field in Recruiting (Requisition or Application templates), invisible to all operators. Name them "Dummy text field for ONB mapping" and "Dummy date field for ONB mapping" (or something similar). Make sure you configure the fields in all requisition or application templates.

Step 2: Run an OData Metadata Refresh to make these fields show in Recruit-To-Hire Data Mapping

Step 3: Map the text field to string or object fields in Onboarding, and the date field to date fields

 

Other topics and recommendations

Specific fields added for Onboarding when you have multiple Recruiting templates

Let's focus on an example. You would like to add two fields, 'Email type' and 'Primary Flag', to the mapping. These fields are not visible to the Recruiter and candidate, and you pre-filled them with a business rule. However, the mapping is not working. One possible reason is that you have added the fields to only one Job Requisition template but there are two templates active and used in Recruiting. In this case, you need to add your fields to all of the templates, because the mapping won't work with one template only .

Pay Component mapping: 

The "amount" should be defined as type "currency" in the offer template. It's only necessary to map the pay component value - not the currency, frequency, or pay component code.

Follow Approach 2 from the KBA 3204900 - [Onboarding] Multiple pay components. The scenario in Approach 2 is the recommended one from release B2111.

Currency - the value is taken from the FO if you don't map it in Recruit-to-Hire Data Mapping. If the value is not available in the FO, then the value for the field is taken from the legal entity. If the value is not available in the legal entity, then the value for the currency-code field is taken from the legal entity country.

Frequency - the values for the Pay Component Recurring and Pay Component Non-Recurring fields are populated from the FO. If not maintained, an error is created in Business Process Engine (BPE).

Alternatively, Currency and Frequency can be set up with business rules in EC.

 

Troubleshooting

A feature to troubleshoot errors in the mapping between Recruiting and Onboarding is available in the Check Tool. Go to Check Tool -> Validation and select "Onboarding" under "Application". Select "Data mapping between Recruiting Management and Onboarding is consistent. (ONB2DataValidationCheckForInitiateOnb)" and then "Run Check". Add the application ID and click "Run". If there are errors in the mapping, the system will provide clear details.

For more details and additional options please review the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/checks-to-troubleshoot-issues-related-to-initiate-onboarding-from/ba-p/13552680

entry.title: Re: aseuserstore and dbisql
entry.content:
Hi,

This app works without username and password, but correct host:port so the property is picked up from the advanced tab.
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE_SDK/c4016b5564ed4c98bbade36a3a35202d/b0822ab9bbf91014ba1bc4fea5c9aafb.html?q=SECURE_STORE_KEY&locale=en-US

Bad host:port will give you:
JZSCF: Conflicting information encountered in secure store.

This behavior is documented and shows how to do this with standalone code:

Example 2

The following example shows the usage of SECURE_STORE_KEY property through a connection URL string. Here, the connection will be established with localhost:1600 if the connection information for ASE1TESTUSER is also specified as localhost:1600, else it results in an error because there is a mismatch between secure store and the connection string.

Code Syntax

DriverManager.getConnection("jdbc:sybase:Tds:localhost:1600?SECURE_STORE_KEY=ASE1TESTUSER);
Example 3
Code Syntax

In the following example, the host and port information is not provided in the connection string. In this scenario, the driver uses the connection information present in the secure store.


DriverManager.getConnection("jdbc:sybase:Tds:?SECURE_STORE_KEY=ASE1TESTUSER);

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE_SDK/c4016b5564ed4c98bbade36a3a35202d/17c13174c0de42fb92adb65ecbe4dc91.html?q=SECURE_STORE_KEY&locale=en-US

I've created a feature request for aseuserstore and dbisql/interactive sql.
Looks like we do not parse or use the alternative url for this product.
CR#830317

Regards,
Ryan

entry.title: Drill-thru to S/4 tcode from a SAC table
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

My customer wants the ability to drill thru from a SAC table to a transaction code (Ex: KSB1) in S/4 using parameters.

 

Is this possible using SAC Planning table? If so, please do share your inputs.

 

Thank you.

 

Regards,

 

Prasad Rangudu

entry.title: How to Use Statistics Functions in SAP PCo
entry.content:
Hello All!

I am currently working on a project using SAP Plant Connectivity (PCo) and would like to understand how to use the Statistics Functions available, such as Integral (time-weighted total), Max, and Min within specific subintervals. (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_PLANT_CONNECTIVITY/c90214be0d934ebdb6f3bce29c63c0ff/0246c884fc27485e85850b177362d5b3.html?q=Integral+(time-weighted+total)

Here’s my current setup:

  • I have both the PI SDK and PI AF SDK installed properly.
  • I am using PI AF as the data source and, in some cases, PI directly as the source system.

However, while exploring the PCo documentation and interface, I couldn’t find where or how to configure and use these statistical functions. My goal is to process historical data and apply these functions to calculate values based on specific time subintervals.

I’d greatly appreciate any guidance or practical examples that the community can share!

Thank you!

I’m incredibly honored and excited to join the SAP Champions family. Can’t wait to contribute and connect with everyone. And congrats to all the new members !

entry.title: Re: Custom Value Help Creation For a Perticular Field In...
entry.content: It seems you created this in the Q&A Section, but you should put in the Blogs by Members section.

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF use Idoc field value as input for a function
entry.content: Hi Pascal, as you are on a 2020 release have a look at the key field navigation feature. https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4db1676c3f114f119b500bd80ccd944d/80f98b77399840b8badfc99c13c2ae68.html?version=202009.latest&locale=en-US It might solve your problem.

entry.title: Do you know how to implement and how to manage SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing and Configuring SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration learning journey.

Overview
This course will guide you through the necessary steps to configure your SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration tenants. It includes setting up the SAP Business Technology Platform and managing necessary master data.​

Learning objectives
This course will prepare you to: 

  • ​Deploy SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration and set up SAP Business Technology Platform
  • ​Configure SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration
  • ​Manage required Master Data​ ​

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the Foundations of SAP Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: aseuserstore and dbisql
entry.content: Thanks for creating ChangeRequest 830317!

entry.title: Re: How to check change logs in table CDHDR and or Utilizing CDHDR and CDPOS SAP Tables for Change
entry.content: In most of the cases, we do not know from which user id, the changes has happened and on which date.  Having said that your screen shot shows only these two fields and if the outcome is only few entries, you cannot straightaway, conclude only those changes has happened.  It is always better to maintain OBJECTCLAS in CDHDR so that data would be fetched at the quickest possible time.

entry.title: Re: The SQL Anywhere for PC Platforms page shows up to Wi...
entry.content: Thank You for the reply.

entry.title: Re: Wildcard Character for WEBi Search
entry.content: Hi Michael, thanks for the reply. Yes, I have tried that combination as well with no luck. My data source is SAP HANA.

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning HUB
entry.content:
Hi @ASAMAD 
Please access our Activation code FAQs 
You find a a detailed answer to the question
How do I redeem my activation code for SAP Learning Hub?
Go to training.sap.com/redeem, enter the activation code and choose “Validate.”
To validate your code, you must have an SAP user account. If you have one, then choose “Log on” and enter your SAP Universal ID. If you don’t have an SAP user account, choose "Create SAP User Account."

For more information on activating your code, see the SAP Activation Code Redemption Guide 

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: where is Crystal Reports Java Reporting Component Dev...
entry.content: not an answer
